summary: web3-secrets-detector ships wallet-scanner and host-intel exfiltration to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages C2
details: The package presents itself as a web3 secrets detector but ships three modules that perform installer-side data collection and exfiltration. scanner.js collects host identity (os.hostname(), username via whoami, execSync output) and POSTs to a hardcoded endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io (lines 142-161, 323), an anonymous GitHub Pages account unrelated to any documented publisher. wallet-scanner.js (1300+ lines) combines fs/child_process/https with multiple POST calls and curl invocations consistent with browser/wallet artifact harvesting and outbound exfiltration. build-verifier.js similarly couples os/fs/https with execSync('docker push...') and POST/https.request calls. The combination of (a) a hardcoded C2 on an anonymous GitHub Pages host, (b) host-identity collection (hostname, username, cwd) bundled into outbound POSTs, and (c) a wallet-targeted scanner module is the active-attack credential/wallet-stealer fingerprint. The package name itself ('secrets-detector') is cover for the inverse behavior — collecting and shipping secrets off-host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
